prevalence.source: Pediatric primary care and STI/URI syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal peaks in school periods

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; risk of rheumatic fever if untreated
burden.source: WHO URI and rheumatic fever context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Centor/McIsaac criteria with rapid antigen test or throat culture to confirm

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis often; rapid testing availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Penicillin/amoxicillin; avoid unnecessary antibiotics for viral pharyngitis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited rapid testing and antibiotic overuse
